assertions.expectation: Defines 'available' as versions officially available in the channel that customers can manually upgrade to
assertions.reason: Response clearly defines available version as 'officially available in your release channel' that 'you can manually upgrade to using gcloud container clusters upgrade, but GKE won't automatically upgrade to it'

assertions.expectation: Defines 'default' as the version used for new cluster creation — typically the same as auto-upgrade target but can differ especially during new minor version introduction (separate promotion stage)
assertions.reason: Response defines default as 'the version used for new cluster creation' and explains they can differ through the progressive rollout system, though it doesn't specifically mention new minor version introduction scenarios

assertions.expectation: Defines 'auto-upgrade target' as the version GKE will actually upgrade existing clusters to automatically — this is cluster-specific based on policies
assertions.reason: Response clearly defines auto-upgrade target as 'the version GKE will actually upgrade your existing cluster to automatically' and mentions it can be affected by maintenance controls and is cluster-specific

assertions.expectation: Explains that default and auto-upgrade target are often the same but CAN differ, especially during new minor version rollouts
assertions.reason: Response explains they can differ through the progressive rollout system and shows an example where default (1.31.8) differs from auto-upgrade target (1.31.7), though doesn't specifically mention minor version rollouts

assertions.expectation: Notes that the auto-upgrade target depends on cluster constraints (maintenance windows, maintenance exclusions) and is cluster-specific — different clusters on the same channel can have different targets
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions that auto-upgrade target can be affected by 'maintenance exclusions', 'release channel', and 'maintenance windows', and notes these are cluster-specific factors

assertions.expectation: Explains that a cluster with a 'no minor' exclusion will have a patch-only auto-upgrade target for its current minor
assertions.reason: Response specifically states 'A no minor exclusion would limit you to patch-only upgrades within 1.31.x' which directly addresses this expectation

assertions.expectation: Recommends using gcloud container clusters get-upgrade-info to check the actual auto-upgrade status, target versions, and EoS timestamps
assertions.reason: Response provides the exact command 'gcloud container clusters get-upgrade-info CLUSTER_NAME --region REGION' and lists what it shows including auto-upgrade target, maintenance controls, and EoS timestamps

assertions.expectation: Points to the GKE release schedule or release notes for tracking version availability
assertions.reason: Response does not mention GKE release schedule or release notes as resources for tracking version availability
